rules.title: String concatenation in loops
rules.summary: Repeated string concatenation in a loop increases allocations and CPU.
rules.rationale: Avoids unnecessary intermediate string objects that compound under load.

impact.notes: Most visible in tight loops / hot paths.

remediation.guidance: Use list accumulation + join(), or StringIO.

rules.title: Unbounded list growth
rules.summary: Collections that grow without bounds increase memory pressure and GC churn.
rules.rationale: Unbounded accumulation is a common cause of avoidable memory spikes and instability.

impact.notes: Often shows up in ETL, batch jobs, API aggregation.

remediation.guidance: Use generators, pagination, batching, or streaming APIs.

rules.title: Repeated invariant computation inside loop
rules.summary: Recomputing values that do not change inside a loop wastes CPU cycles.
rules.rationale: Small wasted work per iteration can become significant in hot loops.

impact.notes: Best targeted when loop counts are high.

remediation.guidance: Move invariant work outside the loop.

rules.title: Blocking I/O inside async context
rules.summary: Blocking calls inside async functions reduce concurrency and inflate latency.
rules.rationale: Blocking I/O defeats the purpose of async and can stall the entire event loop.

remediation.guidance: Use async-compatible libraries (e.g., async DB/HTTP clients).

rules.title: N+1 database query pattern
rules.summary: Queries executed inside iteration multiply round trips and load.
rules.rationale: N+1 is a classic hidden multiplier for latency, cost, and carbon.

remediation.guidance: Use bulk queries, eager loading, or JOINs.
remediation.tradeoffs: May increase memory use; tune carefully.

rules.title: Missing network timeout
rules.summary: Network calls without explicit timeouts can hang and cascade failures.
rules.rationale: Without timeouts, partial outages become thread exhaustion and retry storms.

remediation.guidance: Set explicit connect/read timeouts; prefer client defaults + overrides.

rules.title: Loading entire file into memory
rules.summary: Reading large files fully into memory increases peak RAM and risk of OOM.
rules.rationale: Streaming reduces peak memory and improves stability.

remediation.guidance: Stream/chunk reads; process iteratively.

rules.title: Excessive logging in hot path
rules.summary: Logging in tight loops or request hot paths adds CPU and I/O overhead.
rules.rationale: Hot-path logging is a quiet tax on throughput and latency.

remediation.guidance: Reduce log volume; guard debug logs; sample where appropriate.

rules.title: Repeated regex compilation
rules.summary: Compiling regex repeatedly wastes CPU; compile once and reuse.
rules.rationale: Regex compilation is non-trivial; repeated compilation is wasted work.

remediation.guidance: Precompile regex at module init or reuse compiled patterns.

rules.title: Inefficient data structure choice
rules.summary: Using lists for membership tests instead of sets/dicts increases CPU time.
rules.rationale: O(n) lookups in loops are a common silent perf killer.

impact.notes: High impact when lookups are frequent.

remediation.guidance: Use set/dict for membership-heavy operations.

rules.title: Repeated JSON serialization cycles
rules.summary: Serializing/deserializing repeatedly wastes CPU and increases latency.
rules.rationale: Avoid churn converting structures when you can pass objects directly.

remediation.guidance: Avoid unnecessary encode/decode; serialize once at boundaries.

rules.title: CPU-bound work in request thread
rules.summary: CPU-heavy work in request handlers reduces throughput and increases latency.
rules.rationale: Requests should stay I/O bound when possible; CPU spikes reduce concurrency.

remediation.guidance: Offload CPU work to background jobs or optimize/compile hotspots.

rules.title: Inefficient pandas row iteration
rules.summary: Row-wise pandas iteration is slow compared to vectorized operations.
rules.rationale: Vectorization reduces Python-level overhead dramatically.

remediation.guidance: Use vectorized ops or apply carefully; avoid iterrows in hot paths.

rules.title: Redundant environment variable lookups
rules.summary: Repeated env lookups in hot code paths add overhead and noise.
rules.rationale: Load config once; don’t pay the lookup tax repeatedly.

impact.notes: Small individually; measurable in hot loops.

remediation.guidance: Read env/config once during startup; pass config explicitly.

rules.title: Recreating database connections per request
rules.summary: Creating DB connections per request increases latency and resource churn.
rules.rationale: Connection setup is expensive and can overwhelm DB under load.

remediation.guidance: Use connection pooling and reuse sessions/clients.

rules.title: No connection pooling
rules.summary: Lack of pooling increases connection churn, latency, and DB load.
rules.rationale: Pooling stabilizes performance and reduces wasted setup cost.

remediation.guidance: Enable and tune pooling; set max connections.

rules.title: Large object retained in global scope
rules.summary: Long-lived globals can cause persistent memory bloat.
rules.rationale: Accidental retention prevents GC and inflates baseline memory footprint.

remediation.guidance: Avoid storing large payloads globally; use caching with eviction.

rules.title: Recursive algorithm without safeguards
rules.summary: Recursion without depth safeguards risks overhead and runtime errors.
rules.rationale: Recursion adds call overhead; unbounded recursion risks failure.

remediation.guidance: Consider iterative approaches or enforce depth limits.

rules.title: Excessive thread spawning
rules.summary: Creating many threads increases overhead and contention.
rules.rationale: Thread creation and contention can reduce throughput and increase CPU waste.

impact.notes: Often misused for I/O that could be async.

remediation.guidance: Use thread pools, async I/O, or bounded concurrency.

rules.title: Synchronous subprocess invocation in hot path
rules.summary: Blocking subprocess calls increase latency and consume resources.
rules.rationale: External process calls are expensive and unpredictable under load.

remediation.guidance: Avoid subprocess in request paths; cache results or move to background jobs.

rules.title: Synchronous filesystem calls in request path
rules.summary: Sync FS calls block the event loop and reduce concurrency.
rules.rationale: One blocking FS call can stall all concurrent requests in the same process.

remediation.guidance: Use async fs APIs (promises/callbacks).

rules.title: Polling instead of events
rules.summary: Polling increases unnecessary network traffic and compute.
rules.rationale: Polling wastes cycles when nothing changes; push shifts work to when it matters.

remediation.guidance: Use webhooks, SSE, websockets, or event-driven patterns.

rules.title: Large unoptimized bundles
rules.summary: Large bundles increase transfer size, parse time, and energy use.
rules.rationale: Users pay for bytes and CPU; so do battery and emissions.

remediation.guidance: Enable tree-shaking, code splitting, and remove dead deps.

rules.title: Memory leaks via event listeners
rules.summary: Unremoved listeners retain objects and increase memory over time.
rules.rationale: Leaks increase baseline memory and trigger more GC work.

impact.notes: Often progressive and hard to notice.

remediation.guidance: Remove listeners on cleanup; use weak refs where appropriate.

rules.title: Blocking crypto in event loop
rules.summary: CPU-heavy crypto blocks the event loop and inflates latency.
rules.rationale: Event loop stalls propagate to all concurrent requests.

impact.notes: Use workers for heavy CPU work.

remediation.guidance: Move CPU-heavy crypto to worker threads or native async APIs.

rules.title: Over-fetching API responses
rules.summary: Returning unused fields increases payload size and wasted processing.
rules.rationale: Extra bytes multiply across users and calls.

remediation.guidance: Return only needed fields; use pagination and sparse responses.

rules.title: Missing HTTP caching headers (client-side)
rules.summary: Missing cache headers causes repeated downloads and wasted bandwidth.
rules.rationale: Caching prevents repeated work; missing caching forces it.

remediation.guidance: Set Cache-Control/ETag; use immutable assets with hashes.

rules.title: Excessive DOM reflow
rules.summary: Layout thrashing increases CPU and drains battery.
rules.rationale: Repeated measure/mutate cycles trigger expensive layout work.

remediation.guidance: Batch DOM reads/writes; use requestAnimationFrame patterns.

rules.title: Unbounded promise chains
rules.summary: Long or recursive promise chains can leak work and increase memory usage.
rules.rationale: Unbounded async chains may create retention and scheduling overhead.

remediation.guidance: Bound retries; avoid recursion; use iterative loops with limits.

rules.title: Missing request timeout
rules.summary: Requests without timeouts hang and create cascading latency.
rules.rationale: Timeouts are a reliability boundary; missing them removes the boundary.

remediation.guidance: Use AbortController / client timeouts; set defaults.

rules.title: Inefficient array transformations (multi-pass)
rules.summary: Multiple passes over arrays increases CPU and GC overhead.
rules.rationale: Map/filter/reduce chains can be fine, but in hot paths they add overhead.

remediation.guidance: Combine passes in hot paths; keep readability elsewhere.

rules.title: Redundant API calls in component lifecycle
rules.summary: Repeated fetches on rerender waste network and CPU.
rules.rationale: Chattiness often comes from accidental rerenders and missing caching.

remediation.guidance: Cache results; dedupe requests; stabilize dependencies.

rules.title: Uncompressed static assets
rules.summary: Serving assets without compression increases bandwidth and energy use.
rules.rationale: Compression is one of the simplest bandwidth reductions.

remediation.guidance: Enable gzip/brotli; ensure proper caching.

rules.title: Client-side heavy computation without workers
rules.summary: Heavy CPU work on main thread harms responsiveness and drains battery.
rules.rationale: Main-thread CPU work directly impacts user experience and power use.

remediation.guidance: Move heavy compute to web workers; optimize algorithms.

rules.title: Recreating large objects per render
rules.summary: Allocating large objects repeatedly increases GC churn and CPU.
rules.rationale: Avoid unnecessary allocation churn in rendering loops.

remediation.guidance: Memoize stable objects; move creation outside render paths.
remediation.tradeoffs: Potential for stale data if misused.

rules.title: Excessive object creation in hot path
rules.summary: High allocation rates increase GC pressure and CPU cost.
rules.rationale: Allocation churn increases GC frequency and tail latency.

impact.notes: Often shows up as p99 spikes.

remediation.guidance: Reduce allocations; reuse objects; optimize parsing.

rules.title: Unbounded cache growth
rules.summary: Caches without limits grow until they become the problem.
rules.rationale: Unbounded caches create memory bloat and instability.

remediation.guidance: Use bounded caches with eviction policies and TTL.

rules.title: Thread pool misconfiguration
rules.summary: Incorrect thread pool sizing can waste CPU or cause latency collapse.
rules.rationale: Too many threads = contention; too few = underutilization and queues.

impact.notes: Tune to workload and downstream limits.

remediation.guidance: Tune pools; add backpressure; align with DB/HTTP limits.

rules.title: Reflection in hot path
rules.summary: Reflection adds overhead and can inflate latency and CPU usage.
rules.rationale: Reflection is flexible, but expensive in tight loops.

remediation.guidance: Avoid reflection in hot paths; precompute accessors.

rules.title: N+1 ORM query pattern
rules.summary: ORM queries inside loops multiply DB calls.
rules.rationale: Hidden multiplicative cost for latency and DB load.

remediation.guidance: Use eager fetching, joins, or batch queries.

rules.title: Missing connection pooling
rules.summary: No pooling increases connection churn and DB overhead.
rules.rationale: Connection setup is expensive; pooling stabilizes and reduces waste.

remediation.guidance: Use and tune a pool (e.g., HikariCP).

rules.title: Blocking calls in reactive pipeline
rules.summary: Blocking in reactive code collapses concurrency and throughput.
rules.rationale: Reactive systems rely on non-blocking work to scale efficiently.

remediation.guidance: Use non-blocking APIs; isolate blocking work in bounded schedulers.

rules.title: Excessive synchronization contention
rules.summary: Over-synchronization creates contention and wastes CPU.
rules.rationale: Contention turns CPU into waiting; throughput collapses under load.

remediation.guidance: Reduce lock scope; use concurrent structures; redesign hotspots.

rules.title: Large heap allocation spikes
rules.summary: Heap spikes increase GC pauses and tail latency.
rules.rationale: Large allocations correlate strongly with p99 issues and waste.

remediation.guidance: Reduce allocations; stream processing; tune GC when needed.

rules.title: Debug logging in production hot path
rules.summary: Verbose logs in hot paths waste CPU and I/O.
rules.rationale: Logging can become a dominant cost under high throughput.

remediation.guidance: Lower verbosity; sample; avoid heavy string formatting unless enabled.

rules.title: Missing HTTP caching headers
rules.summary: Missing cache headers causes repeated downloads and wasted work.
rules.rationale: Caching prevents repeated network transfer and repeated compute.

impact.notes: Large impact for static and semi-static responses.

remediation.guidance: Add Cache-Control/ETag; use immutable asset hashing where possible.

rules.title: No gzip/brotli compression
rules.summary: Serving text assets without compression increases bandwidth and energy use.
rules.rationale: Compression is one of the simplest ways to reduce bytes transferred.

remediation.guidance: Enable gzip/brotli at CDN/load balancer/origin.
remediation.tradeoffs: Minimal; watch CPU on origin if no CDN.

rules.title: Chatty microservice communication
rules.summary: Many small calls increase latency and cross-service overhead.
rules.rationale: Chattiness multiplies overhead and failure points.

remediation.guidance: Aggregate requests, introduce caching, or redesign boundaries.
remediation.tradeoffs: May reduce separation or increase payload size.

rules.title: Redundant authentication calls
rules.summary: Repeated auth calls waste CPU and network and add latency.
rules.rationale: Token validation and introspection can become a hidden multiplier.

impact.notes: High when per-request introspection is used.

remediation.guidance: Cache tokens/keys; prefer local validation when safe.

rules.title: Missing timeouts
rules.summary: Missing timeouts remove a critical reliability boundary for network calls.
rules.rationale: Timeouts prevent hangs from turning into cascading failures.

remediation.guidance: Set explicit connect/read timeouts; standardize defaults.

rules.title: No connection reuse (keep-alive disabled)
rules.summary: Disabling keep-alive increases handshake overhead and latency.
rules.rationale: Connection reuse reduces repeated TLS and TCP setup.

remediation.guidance: Enable keep-alive; tune pools and idle timeouts.

rules.title: Excessive retry storms
rules.summary: Aggressive retries amplify failures and increase waste.
rules.rationale: Retries without backoff turn a small outage into a system-wide incident.

remediation.guidance: Use exponential backoff + jitter; add circuit breakers; cap retries.
remediation.tradeoffs: May reduce immediate success rate during blips.

rules.title: Over-fetching API fields
rules.summary: Returning unnecessary fields increases payload size and processing.
rules.rationale: Extra bytes multiply across calls and users.

remediation.guidance: Use sparse fieldsets; avoid sending unused data.

rules.title: Under-fetching causing follow-up calls
rules.summary: Responses missing needed data cause extra round trips.
rules.rationale: Follow-up calls are hidden multiplicative cost.

remediation.guidance: Batch endpoints; design responses around common usage.

rules.title: Large payloads without pagination
rules.summary: Large unpaginated responses increase memory and bandwidth waste.
rules.rationale: Huge responses create spikes and slow clients and servers.

remediation.guidance: Introduce pagination, streaming, or filtering.

rules.title: No CDN usage for static content
rules.summary: Serving static content from origin increases latency and origin load.
rules.rationale: CDNs reduce transfer distance and offload repeated work from origin.

remediation.guidance: Use a CDN for static assets and cacheable endpoints.

rules.title: No HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 where applicable
rules.summary: Older HTTP versions may reduce efficiency for multiplexed workloads.
rules.rationale: Multiplexing and improved transport can reduce overhead and latency.

impact.notes: Depends on traffic patterns and client support.

remediation.guidance: Enable HTTP/2+ on TLS endpoints; evaluate HTTP/3 at edge.

rules.title: Excessive polling intervals
rules.summary: Frequent polling increases load even when nothing changes.
rules.rationale: A million clients polling becomes a distributed denial-of-wallet.

remediation.guidance: Use push mechanisms; increase intervals; add caching and ETags.

rules.title: Synchronous cross-region calls
rules.summary: Cross-region synchronous calls increase latency and cost.
rules.rationale: Distance multiplies latency and increases transfer cost.

remediation.guidance: Co-locate dependencies; async replicate; cache at edges.

rules.title: Missing circuit breaker patterns
rules.summary: Without circuit breakers, failures propagate and waste resources.
rules.rationale: Circuit breakers prevent repeated failing calls and protect upstream capacity.

remediation.guidance: Implement circuit breakers; define fallbacks and budgets.

rules.title: Over-provisioned compute
rules.summary: Sustained low utilization suggests oversized instances or replicas.
rules.rationale: Over-provisioning is continuous waste: cost and emissions without benefit.

remediation.guidance: Right-size; reduce replicas; add autoscaling with guardrails.

rules.title: Always-on low-traffic service
rules.summary: Services running 24/7 with low utilization create baseline waste.
rules.rationale: Idle systems still consume energy and operational attention.

impact.notes: High if many services are idle.

remediation.guidance: Evaluate scale-to-zero, serverless, or consolidation.

rules.title: Long synchronous dependency chain
rules.summary: Synchronous call chains amplify latency and failure propagation.
rules.rationale: Chains multiply tail latency and increase coupled failure modes.

remediation.guidance: Introduce async boundaries, caching, or collapse hops.

rules.title: No autoscaling policy
rules.summary: Without scaling policies, systems drift into waste or fragility.
rules.rationale: Static sizing is rarely correct across demand variability.

remediation.guidance: Add autoscaling with SLO guardrails and max limits.

rules.title: No resource limits in containers
rules.summary: Missing CPU/memory limits causes noisy-neighbor waste and instability.
rules.rationale: Limits are a boundary; without them, one workload can consume the cluster.

remediation.guidance: Set requests/limits; right-size with metrics.

rules.title: Unbounded message queues
rules.summary: Queues without bounds hide backpressure and create runaway cost.
rules.rationale: Unbounded queues become time bombs: cost, latency, and failure cascades.

remediation.guidance: Add backpressure, caps, DLQs, and shedding strategies.

rules.title: Batch jobs run too frequently
rules.summary: Over-scheduling batch jobs wastes compute and increases cost.
rules.rationale: Many batch workloads run far more often than the business needs.

remediation.guidance: Right-size schedules; trigger on events; add change-detection.

rules.title: Hot storage used for cold data
rules.summary: Keeping cold data in hot tiers wastes storage spend and energy.
rules.rationale: Tiering aligns cost and energy with access patterns.

remediation.guidance: Introduce lifecycle policies and cold tiers.

rules.title: Duplicate services performing the same work
rules.summary: Duplicate services increase operational load and waste compute.
rules.rationale: Duplication multiplies deploy, run, monitor, and patch overhead.

remediation.guidance: Consolidate responsibilities; retire redundant services.

rules.title: No observability on utilization
rules.summary: Without utilization metrics, waste is invisible and persistent.
rules.rationale: You can’t reduce what you don’t see.

remediation.guidance: Instrument CPU/mem/QPS/latency; add dashboards and alerts.

rules.title: Excessive replica counts
rules.summary: Too many replicas increase baseline waste.
rules.rationale: Replica counts often drift upward and never return.

remediation.guidance: Reduce replicas and add autoscaling with guardrails.

rules.title: No graceful degradation strategy
rules.summary: Without degradation, overload becomes failure and waste.
rules.rationale: Graceful degradation prevents overload collapse and retry storms.

remediation.guidance: Define shedding, fallbacks, and priority paths.

rules.title: No caching layer for high-read workloads
rules.summary: High-read systems without caching waste CPU and DB capacity.
rules.rationale: Caching reduces repeated work and improves latency.

remediation.guidance: Introduce caching at appropriate boundaries.

rules.title: Stateful services blocking scaling
rules.summary: Stateful designs make scaling expensive and fragile.
rules.rationale: State creates coupling that increases baseline and peak waste.

remediation.guidance: Externalize state; use managed stores; reduce coupling.

rules.title: Tight coupling across bounded contexts
rules.summary: Coupling increases coordination cost and failure propagation.
rules.rationale: Coupling drives accidental complexity and multiplies work.

remediation.guidance: Clarify boundaries; reduce synchronous dependencies.

rules.title: Multi-tenant workloads without isolation
rules.summary: Lack of isolation causes noisy-neighbor waste and instability.
rules.rationale: Without isolation you overprovision to avoid incidents.

remediation.guidance: Add isolation, quotas, and per-tenant limits.

rules.title: Inefficient container image size
rules.summary: Large images increase pull time and wasted storage/transfer.
rules.rationale: Slim images reduce bandwidth and speed deploys.

remediation.guidance: Use multi-stage builds and minimal base images.

rules.title: No cold-start optimization
rules.summary: Cold starts inflate latency and may force overprovisioning.
rules.rationale: Poor cold starts often lead teams to keep things always-on.

remediation.guidance: Reduce init work; warm pools selectively; cache dependencies.

rules.title: Overly aggressive autoscaling thresholds
rules.summary: Aggressive scaling can cause thrash and wasted churn.
rules.rationale: Thrash wastes compute and destabilizes latency.

remediation.guidance: Add stabilization windows and sane thresholds.

rules.title: Underutilized GPU/accelerator resources
rules.summary: Accelerators running idle waste significant power and cost.
rules.rationale: GPU idle burn is expensive; schedule and batch workloads.

remediation.guidance: Consolidate workloads; enable autoscaling; batch inference.

rules.title: Duplicate stored data
rules.summary: Redundant data increases storage footprint and cost.
rules.rationale: Duplication silently accumulates and multiplies downstream processing.

impact.notes: Often found in pipelines and analytics copies.

remediation.guidance: Deduplicate; normalize; define canonical sources.

rules.title: Missing retention policy
rules.summary: No TTL/lifecycle policy causes unbounded data growth.
rules.rationale: Without retention, storage becomes the default memory of the organization.

remediation.guidance: Define retention tiers and lifecycle automation.

rules.title: Large unused indexes
rules.summary: Unused indexes waste storage and slow writes.
rules.rationale: Indexes are not free; they multiply storage and write cost.

remediation.guidance: Drop truly unused indexes; reassess queries.

rules.title: Logs stored indefinitely
rules.summary: Indefinite log retention increases storage and cost without clear value.
rules.rationale: Logging without boundaries is a storage leak at organizational scale.

impact.notes: Often a top cost line item.

remediation.guidance: Set retention and tiering; sample where appropriate.

rules.title: Full table scans without index
rules.summary: Full scans increase CPU, IO, and latency for queries.
rules.rationale: Scans scale poorly and waste compute as data grows.

remediation.guidance: Add appropriate indexes; rewrite queries; partition large tables.

rules.title: Excessive replication factor
rules.summary: High replication increases storage and write amplification.
rules.rationale: Replication should match durability needs, not fear.

remediation.guidance: Right-size replication; use tiered durability patterns.

rules.title: No partitioning for large tables
rules.summary: Large tables without partitioning lead to expensive queries and maintenance.
rules.rationale: Partitioning reduces scan scope and improves manageability.

remediation.guidance: Partition by time/key; enforce pruning; archive older partitions.

rules.title: Storing ephemeral data permanently
rules.summary: Ephemeral data kept forever becomes waste by default.
rules.rationale: Temporary data should expire; permanence requires justification.

remediation.guidance: Add TTL/expiration and lifecycle policies.

rules.title: No archival tier strategy
rules.summary: Lack of archival tiering keeps costs and energy higher than necessary.
rules.rationale: Archival tiers align cost with access patterns.

remediation.guidance: Adopt tiering and retrieval policies.

rules.title: Overly aggressive replication across regions
rules.summary: Cross-region replication can add cost and complexity beyond needs.
rules.rationale: Replication should follow RPO/RTO requirements, not defaults.

remediation.guidance: Right-size DR and replication strategies.

rules.title: Lack of compression in object storage
rules.summary: Uncompressed objects waste storage and bandwidth.
rules.rationale: Compression reduces stored bytes and transfer cost.

remediation.guidance: Compress at write; enforce content-encoding where appropriate.

rules.title: Unbounded analytics queries
rules.summary: Unbounded queries cause runaway compute and unpredictable cost.
rules.rationale: Analytics engines will happily burn money unless bounded.

impact.notes: Common in BI and ad-hoc SQL.

remediation.guidance: Add quotas, limits, and guardrails; require partition filters.

rules.title: No data lifecycle governance
rules.summary: Lack of lifecycle governance leads to perpetual growth and shadow datasets.
rules.rationale: Without governance, retention becomes accidental and expensive.

remediation.guidance: Define lifecycle owners, policies, and audits.

rules.title: Stale feature flags accumulating
rules.summary: Feature flags left indefinitely add complexity and runtime overhead.
rules.rationale: Stale flags increase cognitive load and sometimes runtime branching costs.

remediation.guidance: Set expiration dates and cleanup workflows.

rules.title: Shadow data stores outside governance
rules.summary: Unofficial copies create duplicated storage and compliance risk.
rules.rationale: Shadow stores multiply cost and make lifecycle impossible to enforce.

remediation.guidance: Inventory data stores; establish canonical sources and policies.

rules.title: Oversized model selection
rules.summary: Using larger models than needed increases inference cost and emissions.
rules.rationale: Model size is a direct multiplier for compute and energy.

remediation.guidance: Benchmark smaller models and quantify tradeoffs before scaling.

rules.title: No inference batching
rules.summary: No batching increases per-request overhead and lowers throughput.
rules.rationale: Batching improves throughput and reduces overhead per token/request.

remediation.guidance: Introduce batching at gateway/inference server.
remediation.tradeoffs: Added latency for low volume; needs tuning.

rules.title: Re-embedding unchanged data
rules.summary: Recomputing embeddings for unchanged inputs wastes compute.
rules.rationale: Embeddings should be cached based on content hash/version.

remediation.guidance: Cache embeddings; invalidate on content change only.

rules.title: No prompt caching
rules.summary: Repeated prompts without caching waste tokens and compute.
rules.rationale: Many prompts are repetitive; caching reduces repeated work.

remediation.guidance: Cache deterministic responses with TTL and safe scoping.

rules.title: Always-on inference endpoints
rules.summary: Always-on endpoints waste baseline compute when idle.
rules.rationale: Idle accelerators still burn power; scale needs boundaries.

remediation.guidance: Autoscale; scale-to-zero where feasible; consolidate endpoints.

rules.title: Unbounded context window usage
rules.summary: Excessive context increases token cost and latency.
rules.rationale: Token usage is a direct multiplier for cost and energy.

remediation.guidance: Summarize, retrieve selectively, and cap context intentionally.

rules.title: No model quantization
rules.summary: Failure to quantize when appropriate wastes inference compute.
rules.rationale: Quantization can reduce compute cost with acceptable quality tradeoffs.

remediation.guidance: Evaluate quantization for inference workloads.

rules.title: Re-training without drift detection
rules.summary: Training without drift checks wastes compute and introduces risk.
rules.rationale: Training should be triggered by need, not habit.

remediation.guidance: Add drift detection and training triggers.

rules.title: No evaluation before scaling model
rules.summary: Scaling without evaluation wastes resources and can degrade outcomes.
rules.rationale: Scale should follow evidence, not excitement.

remediation.guidance: Require eval gates before scaling model size/traffic.

rules.title: Overly frequent fine-tuning cycles
rules.summary: Frequent tuning without clear value wastes compute.
rules.rationale: Tuning cycles should be justified by measurable benefit.

remediation.guidance: Set tuning cadence based on outcomes and drift.

rules.title: Storing all embeddings indefinitely
rules.summary: Embedding stores without retention grow unbounded and expensive.
rules.rationale: Embeddings are data; they need lifecycle rules too.

remediation.guidance: Apply retention and tiering; delete stale embeddings.

rules.title: Large model in low-SLA workload
rules.summary: Using high-cost models where latency/quality needs are modest wastes resources.
rules.rationale: Model choice should match workload needs, not default to maximum.

remediation.guidance: Tier models by use case; route requests by need.

rules.title: No GPU utilization monitoring
rules.summary: Without GPU utilization metrics, accelerator waste stays invisible.
rules.rationale: You can’t right-size accelerators without seeing utilization.

remediation.guidance: Add GPU metrics, dashboards, and alerts.

rules.title: Inefficient feature preprocessing pipelines
rules.summary: Preprocessing waste increases training and inference cost.
rules.rationale: Preprocessing is often a hidden cost multiplier.

remediation.guidance: Cache features; vectorize; batch transforms.

rules.title: No batching of vector search queries
rules.summary: Unbatched vector queries increase overhead and reduce throughput.
rules.rationale: Batching reduces per-request overhead for retrieval.

remediation.guidance: Batch retrieval queries where possible.

rules.title: No performance budget defined
rules.summary: Without explicit budgets, performance and efficiency drift.
rules.rationale: Budgets make efficiency intentional instead of accidental.

remediation.guidance: Define performance budgets per service and user journey.

rules.title: No baseline measurement
rules.summary: Without baseline data, improvements can’t be validated.
rules.rationale: Baseline-first prevents wasted optimization cycles and false wins.

remediation.guidance: Establish baselines before optimization; track before/after.

rules.title: No cost observability
rules.summary: Without cost attribution, waste persists unmanaged.
rules.rationale: If teams can’t see cost per service, they can’t act on it.

remediation.guidance: Implement cost allocation, tagging, and service-level chargeback/showback.

rules.title: No carbon awareness
rules.summary: Without carbon signals, teams can’t optimize responsibly.
rules.rationale: Carbon awareness is an input to better tradeoffs, not moral theater.

remediation.guidance: Add carbon estimates to reporting; tie to decision points.

rules.title: Feature shipped without load testing
rules.summary: Skipping load tests creates risk and often forces wasteful overprovisioning.
rules.rationale: Without tests, teams overprovision “just in case.”

remediation.guidance: Add load tests and regression gates tied to SLOs.

rules.title: No lifecycle data policy
rules.summary: Missing lifecycle policies cause unbounded storage and compliance risk.
rules.rationale: Lifecycle policies keep data intentional and bounded.

remediation.guidance: Define retention/archival/deletion policies and owners.

rules.title: No energy-aware CI/CD metrics
rules.summary: Build/test pipelines can waste large amounts of compute when unmeasured.
rules.rationale: CI is often a hidden always-on compute bill.

remediation.guidance: Measure build minutes, cache hits, and pipeline efficiency; reduce waste.

rules.title: No architectural review gate
rules.summary: Without review gates, high-propagation waste slips in unnoticed.
rules.rationale: High-impact decisions deserve lightweight review and documentation.

remediation.guidance: Add a lightweight architecture review for high-tier changes.

rules.title: No dependency lifecycle management
rules.summary: Unmanaged dependencies increase security, compute, and maintenance waste.
rules.rationale: Old deps increase risk and often block efficiency upgrades.

remediation.guidance: Implement dependency review cadence and upgrade workflows.

rules.title: No hardware refresh sustainability policy
rules.summary: Hardware lifecycle without sustainability policy increases e-waste risk.
rules.rationale: Hardware policies shape embodied emissions and waste streams.

remediation.guidance: Define repair/reuse/recycle standards and vendor requirements.

rules.title: No decommissioning workflow
rules.summary: Without decommissioning, dead systems stay alive and waste resources.
rules.rationale: Zombie services are pure waste: cost, risk, and attention.

remediation.guidance: Create a decommission playbook and ownership model.

rules.title: No SLO-based scaling validation
rules.summary: Scaling without SLO validation often leads to overprovisioning or fragility.
rules.rationale: SLOs turn scaling from guesswork into evidence.

remediation.guidance: Define SLOs and validate scaling changes against them.

rules.title: No capacity planning cadence
rules.summary: Without planning, teams overbuy or get surprised and scramble.
rules.rationale: Planning reduces panic-driven overprovisioning.

remediation.guidance: Add regular capacity review using trends and forecasts.

rules.title: No energy-efficient coding standards
rules.summary: Without standards, teams repeat avoidable inefficiencies.
rules.rationale: Standards reduce repeated mistakes and speed reviews.

remediation.guidance: Publish standards and integrate them into reviews and tooling.

rules.title: No sustainability accountability owner
rules.summary: Without ownership, sustainability work becomes optional and inconsistent.
rules.rationale: Ownership creates continuity and turns intent into action.

remediation.guidance: Assign clear ownership and decision rights for sustainability metrics.
